name: Joyce's Indonesian Fried Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the tamarind pulp with ½ cup of boiling water. Use the back of a fork to mash the fibers and seeds. When dissolved, strain and reserve ⅓ cup of tamarind water.
2. Process or pound shallots, garlic, chiles, shrimp paste, turmeric, and salt into a smooth paste in a food processor or mortar.
3. Heat a wok or skillet over medium-high heat until hot. Add the oil and spice paste; gently brown.
4. Reduce heat to high and add the shrimp. Stir-fry until they turn bright orange, about 30 seconds. Remove and set aside.
5. Add bell peppers, peas, and cabbage; stir-fry until vegetables are cooked but still crisp, about 1½ minutes.
6. Add rice; stir-fry together, breaking up the lumps of rice.
7. When the rice grains are separated, add tamarind water, ketjap manis, light soy sauce, green onions, chicken, barbecued pork, and reserved shrimp. Mix thoroughly.
8. Check for seasonings. Transfer to a serving plate, garnish with coriander, and arrange cucumbers around the edge.
